title: SHAP for feature diagnosis
scope: Modern attribution; recency hook. Explains SHAP as the contemporary standard for understanding both global and local feature contributions, including directional impact.
key_concepts: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) is based on cooperative game theory and explains individual predictions.
Unlike global permutation importance, SHAP shows the exact direction (positive/negative) and magnitude of a feature's effect on a single specific prediction.
Aggregating local SHAP values provides a comprehensive global view of feature behavior across the dataset.
definitions: SHAP: A method to explain individual predictions by computing the contribution of each feature to the prediction, compared to the average model output.
examples: While permutation importance shows 'age' is important, SHAP reveals that an age over 50 increases the predicted risk by 12% for patient A, but decreases it by 2% for patient B due to interactions with other features.
relations_to_other_chapters: Builds on previous global metrics (s04) by offering local interpretability.
what_to_exclude: The exact mathematical Shapley value formula and combinatorial calculation.
